response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid full-stack experience with strong geospatial credentials via GISCollective and Open Green Map work, plus low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as primary skills, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (2014-2015) with no recent systems-level work — primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Worth a maybe given the geospatial background and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks, but the C++/Rust gap is significant.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS experience via GISCollective and Green Map platform development
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust experience — only brief C/C++ stint a decade ago at National Instruments
Primary recent stack is web (TypeScript/Node.js), not systems/pipeline engineering
No explicit experience with routing tiles, map data pipelines, or navigation guidance systems

key_factors.factor: Low-latency distributed systems experience (DLang at Sociomantic)
